intro: Hieracium pseudofritzei, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pseudofritzei include sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium pseudofritzei is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, skin problems, and infections.
